_source: TH_EXPLORE_IT_DATA.json (authoritative structured data). Real accessions, per-item licence + open_access flag. Nothing authored by an assistant.

station.tagline: She was first to sail past every horizon — and first to feel how lonely that is.

museum_objects.title: Escudo Founding Decree-Law Documentation
museum_objects.description: Documentation of the decree-law that created the Portuguese escudo, replacing the real just after the fall of the monarchy.
museum_objects.significance: Ana was born the same year Portugal became a republic — she carries the weight of being the first currency of a country reinventing itself entirely.
museum_objects.museum: Museu do Dinheiro, Banco de Portugal
museum_objects.plain_language: Records of the 1911 law that created Portugal's escudo, right after the country overthrew its monarchy and became a republic.
museum_objects.institution: Museu do Dinheiro, Banco de Portugal

museum_objects.title: Portuguese Caravel Model
museum_objects.description: A detailed model of a Portuguese caravel, the ship type that powered the Age of Discovery centuries before Ana's birth.
museum_objects.significance: Ana inherited a nation's entire identity as history's great explorers — she was 'first' before she was even born, and that legacy never let her rest.
museum_objects.plain_language: A detailed wooden model of the ships Portuguese explorers used centuries ago to become the first Europeans to sail around Africa and reach Asia.

resolved.via: long tail — needs a per-domain licence rule

display_alternatives.title: Portuguese caravel, Swiss National Museum, Zurich, Switzerland julesvernex2.jpg

museum_objects.rights_note: CITED: the original object (rights unclear/blocked). DISPLAYED: an openly-licensed image of the same kind of object, credited to its own institution. We cite one and show the other, and we say so.

museum_objects.title: Portuguese Caravel Model (Royal Museums Greenwich)
museum_objects.description: Another detailed caravel model held in the UK's premier maritime museum, illustrating the ship design that enabled Portuguese exploration.
museum_objects.significance: Even a British museum keeps a model of the ships that made Ana's ancestors the first in the world to chart new oceans.
museum_objects.plain_language: A model of a Portuguese exploration ship, kept in a British maritime museum because of how influential these ships were.

resolved.via: long tail — needs a per-domain licence rule

museum_objects.rights_note: NO OPEN IMAGE FOUND — panel must show text/citation only.

museum_objects.title: Jerónimos Monastery (UNESCO Site)
museum_objects.description: A monumental monastery in Lisbon built to commemorate Vasco da Gama's voyage to India, funded partly by the spice trade.
museum_objects.significance: This building is what Ana's early economic power actually looked like — spice-trade wealth turned into limestone monuments to a golden age.
museum_objects.plain_language: A stunning monastery in Lisbon built with wealth from Portugal's spice trade after its explorers found new routes to Asia.

resolved.via: long tail — needs a per-domain licence rule

museum_objects.title: Lisbon Photographs (1910-1926)
museum_objects.description: A photographic collection documenting Lisbon during the exact period of the Portuguese Republic's founding and Ana's birth.
museum_objects.significance: These are the actual streets Ana was born onto — a capital in the middle of political upheaval, still carrying centuries of maritime pride.
museum_objects.plain_language: Old photographs of Lisbon from right around the time Portugal became a republic and the escudo was created.

world_map.map_description: Ana's escudo was born from the fall of the Portuguese monarchy and carried the legacy of the Age of Discovery until its retirement for the euro in 2002.

nodes.label: Lisbon (Museu do Dinheiro)
nodes.description: Portugal's capital, where the escudo was established in 1911 and where its history is preserved today.

nodes.label: Belém Tower / Jerónimos Monastery
nodes.description: Iconic monuments built with wealth from the Age of Discovery, symbolizing Portugal's maritime golden age.

nodes.label: Sagres Navigation School
nodes.description: Legendary site associated with Prince Henry the Navigator's efforts to advance Portuguese maritime exploration.

nodes.label: Angola
nodes.description: Former Portuguese colony whose 1975 independence marked a major turning point in the escudo's colonial-era economic reach.

nodes.label: Mozambique
nodes.description: Another former Portuguese colony, part of the broader decolonization that reshaped the escudo's economic footprint.
